summary: Tested Operator Comparison Dashboard (backend + frontend). Backend endpoint /api/analytics/admin/operator-comparison works perfectly (6/6 pytest tests pass: 2 ops, 3 ops, invalid 1, invalid 4, operator role 403, unauthenticated 403). Frontend page renders for admin role at /admin/operator-comparison with all data-testids (page, slots 0/1, add button, period selector, 3rd slot on click). However, the operator selection dropdown is EMPTY because /operators/?limit=200 returns HTTP 422 (backend caps limit at 100). This blocks the user from picking operators, so the comparison cards/chart/category-table flow could not be exercised end-to-end through UI. Note: frontend needed a manual supervisor restart for Vite to register the newly-added route /admin/operator-comparison — before restart, navigation fell through to the wildcard route and redirected admins to /admin/admin-dashboard.

ui_bugs.issue: Operator catalog fetch uses api.get('/operators/?limit=200') but the backend /api/operators endpoint enforces limit<=100 → returns HTTP 422 → catch block sets allOperators=[] → Operator 1/2/3 dropdowns are empty → the entire comparison flow is unreachable through the UI. Change limit to 100 (or paginate). This is the blocker for the feature in the browser.
When the operator catalog call fails, the page silently falls back to empty list with no error toast/banner. User has no feedback on why slots are empty. Recommend showing an inline error.
Console warns 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on first paint when sidebar layout is collapsing/expanding. Cosmetic, but worth a minHeight on the chart wrapper.

integration_issues.issue: Picker never populates because of the limit=200 validation 422, so the downstream /analytics/admin/operator-comparison call is never triggered from the UI (though it works fine via direct API).

action_items: Fix OperatorComparison.jsx: change api.get('/operators/?limit=200') → '/operators/?limit=100' (or implement pagination if >100 operators expected). This is the only blocker.
Optional: surface an error toast when the operator catalog fetch fails so the empty dropdown isn't silent.
Optional: give the ResponsiveContainer a minHeight to silence the recharts width(-1) warning.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend hot-reload did not pick up the new <Route path="/admin/operator-comparison"> until supervisor restart — Vite occasionally needs a kick when new top-level imports are added. Not a code bug, but worth knowing on next dev cycle.
Backend endpoint correctly enforces 2–3 operator_ids and the analytics.view_dashboard permission. operator@test.com returns 403 as expected.
OperatorComparison.jsx swallows the operator catalog error with `catch { setAllOperators([]); }` — this is what's masking the 422. Consider logging or surfacing the error.
buildCategoryRows + mergedChart memoization is clean. KPI 'leader' computation correctly uses max() with -Infinity seed.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (page + controls render and respond, but data flow blocked by /operators/?limit=200 → 422)

test_credentials: admin@test.com / testpassword123; superadmin@oryno.com / testpassword123; operator@test.com / testpassword123
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Carter / Musango / Netflix operator IDs supplied in the request.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the limit=200 → 100 issue in OperatorComparison.jsx, retest the full UI flow: pick Carter + Musango → cards render with Revenue/Orders/AOV/Completion Rate + Trophy on leader → chart card + category table render → change period selector → add third operator (Netflix). Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_operator_comparison.py already covers permission + 2/3-op validation; rerun it as regression. Beware /api/auth/login 429 rate limit — wait ~60–75s between bursts.
rca of the issue: Root cause: OperatorComparison.jsx requests `/operators/?limit=200`, but the backend Query validator on /api/operators caps limit at 100 (le=100), so the response is HTTP 422 with body {"detail":[{"type":"less_than_equal","loc":["query","limit"],"msg":"Input should be less than or equal to 100"}]}. The frontend's try/catch silently converts that to an empty operator list, leaving the Select dropdowns empty. Reproduction: 1) login as admin, 2) visit /admin/operator-comparison, 3) open Operator 1 dropdown — 0 options. Mitigation: change limit to 100 in OperatorComparison.jsx (single-line fix) or relax the backend cap. Additional note: the new route `/admin/operator-comparison` required a supervisor restart of the frontend before Vite served it; the wildcard route was previously redirecting admins to /admin/admin-dashboard.
